figures.caption: Table 6: Unified fact-level evaluation on YouCook3-Fact. F+ g denotes the annotated positive facts from the gold captions, while F− g refers to the generated negative facts constructed to contradict F+ g . Fp represents the facts extracted from the model-generated captions. V and C indicate the video clip and gold caption used as input for verification.

Table 1: Taxonomy of factual errors in video captioning. Hallucination introduces unsupported content, Salience misattributes visible but irrelevant elements, and Omission excludes task-relevant information.

Table 8: Unified fact-level evaluation on CraftBench-Fact. F+ g denotes the annotated positive facts from the gold captions, while F− g refers to the generated negative facts constructed to contradict F+ g . Fp represents the facts extracted from the model-generated captions. V and C indicate the video clip and gold caption used as input for verification.

Figure 1: Overview of the MULTIFACTSCORE pipeline. The captioning model generates a description, from which positive facts are extracted and verified using multimodal and textual NLI models. Error decomposition identifies hallucination, salience, and omission errors for fine-grained factuality analysis.
